examples.title: Algorithm: Android App Initialization
examples.description: Core steps to create basic Android application structure
examples.code:
ALGORITHM CreateFirstApp
BEGIN
 1. Initialize project in Android Studio
 2. Select Empty Activity template
 3. Configure project (name, package, language)
 4. Wait for Gradle build completion
 5. Modify activity_main.xml layout
 6. Implement MainActivity class
 7. Build APK and deploy to emulator
END
examples.explanation:
Steps 1-4: Project setup
Step 5: UI definition
Step 6: Activity lifecycle hook
Step 7: Deployment process

examples.title: MainActivity Implementation
examples.description: Basic Activity class with onCreate() lifecycle method
examples.explanation:
Line 3: Mandatory superclass call
Line 4: Links Java code to XML layout
R.layout: Auto-generated resources reference

examples.title: activity_main.xml Layout
examples.code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<androidx.constraintlayout.widget.ConstraintLayout
 xmlns:android="http://schemas.android.com/apk/res/android"
 xmlns:app="http://schemas.android.com/apk/res-auto"
 android:layout_width="match_parent"
 android:layout_height="match_parent">

 <TextView
 android:id="@+id/myTextView"
 android:layout_width="wrap_content"
 android:layout_height="wrap_content"
 android:text="Welcome to university!"
 app:layout_constraintBottom_toBottomOf="parent"
 app:layout_constraintLeft_toLeftOf="parent"
 app:layout_constraintRight_toRightOf="parent"
 app:layout_constraintTop_toTopOf="parent" />
</androidx.constraintlayout.widget.ConstraintLayout>
examples.explanation:
ConstraintLayout: Modern flexible layout
@+id: Resource ID creation
constraint attributes: Center positioning
